title: coleogeton filiformis subsp. alpinus
intro: Coleogeton filiformis subsp. alpinus, also known as the alpine Coleogeton, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Aponogetonaceae, typically found in the mountainous regions of New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The alpine Coleogeton is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. It can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion or decoction, often consumed as a tea or added to other medicinal preparations.
